name: React Native
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in React Native (3-5 years experience) should be able to architect, build, and maintain complex mobile applications with well-structured codebases. They understand advanced navigation patterns including nested navigators (Stack inside Tab, Drawer with Stack), deep linking configuration, conditional navigation flows (auth vs main), and navigation state persistence. They can design and implement scalable state management using Context API with useReducer for global state, understand when to reach for external libraries (Redux Toolkit, Zustand), and implement patterns like optimistic updates and normalized state shapes. They are proficient with performance optimization techniques including FlatList tuning (getItemLayout, windowSize, maxToRenderPerBatch, removeClippedSubviews), React.memo for preventing unnecessary re-renders, useMemo and useCallback for expensive computations and stable references, and can use React DevTools Profiler to identify bottlenecks. They can build custom hooks that encapsulate complex business logic (data fetching with caching, form management, device sensors) and compose hooks for reusable patterns. They implement robust API integration with proper error handling, retry logic, request cancellation, pagination (infinite scroll with onEndReached), and pull-to-refresh patterns. They understand the React Native Animated API for creating smooth animations (timing, spring, parallel, sequence) and can use LayoutAnimation for implicit animations. They handle platform-specific code effectively using Platform.OS, Platform.select, and platform-specific file extensions (.ios.js, .android.js). They implement proper error boundaries with fallback UI, graceful degradation for network failures, and offline-capable features using AsyncStorage for data persistence and caching. They understand and apply component architecture patterns including compound components, render props adapted for mobile, presentational vs container separation, and proper prop typing. They can configure and use Expo's managed workflow effectively including environment configuration, asset management, and common Expo SDK modules (expo-image-picker, expo-location, expo-notifications). They participate in code reviews focusing on mobile performance, accessibility, and architecture decisions.
